summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is a 'context' description when describing data?

Context means relating the statistics back to what the data represents, such as saying 'the typical test score is 82' rather than just 'the median is 82.'

A bakery tracked daily donut sales over a month. The data is left-skewed with a median of 120 and an IQR of 25. What is the best description of a typical day's sales?

The median of 120 gives the center and the IQR of 25 means Q1 is about 120 - 12.5 = 107.5 and Q3 is about 120 + 12.5 = 132.5. This describes a typical day using context.

summary_sections.title: Outliers and Resistant Measures
summary_sections.content_html:
Outliers and Resistant Measures

A non-resistant measure is one that is strongly influenced by extreme values. The mean changes significantly when outliers are present because it uses every data value in its calculation.

A complete description includes shape (left-skewed due to low outliers), center (around 85), spread, and unusual features (two outliers near 40).

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: Which of the following is an example of an unusual feature in a distribution?

Unusual features include outliers, gaps, and clusters that stand out from the overall pattern of the data.

Example: A fitness tracker company collected step counts for 100 users on a typical weekday. The histogram shows a right-skewed distribution with a median of 6,500 steps and a mean of 7,800 steps. The company wants to advertise the 'average' user's step count. Which value should they use and why?

In a right-skewed distribution, a few very active users inflate the mean. The median of 6,500 better represents the typical user since it is resistant to those extreme values.

Example: A school compared test scores between two classes. Class A: symmetric, mean = 78, SD = 5. Class B: symmetric, mean = 78, SD = 12. Which class performed more consistently?

Both classes have the same center (mean = 78), but Class A's smaller standard deviation (5 vs. 12) means its scores cluster more tightly around 78, indicating more consistent performance.

Example: A real estate agent looks at home prices in two neighborhoods. Neighborhood A: median $250,000, IQR $30,000. Neighborhood B: median $245,000, IQR $120,000. A buyer wants predictable pricing. Which neighborhood should the agent recommend?

Neighborhood A has a smaller IQR ($30,000 vs. $120,000), meaning home prices are more predictable and consistent. The medians are similar, so the key difference is spread.

Example: A bakery tracked daily donut sales over a month. The data is left-skewed with a median of 120 and an IQR of 25. What is the best description of a typical day's sales?

The median of 120 gives the center and the IQR of 25 means Q1 is about 120 - 12.5 = 107.5 and Q3 is about 120 + 12.5 = 132.5. This describes a typical day using context.

Example: A teacher notices that one class has test scores clustered tightly around 85 with two outliers at 40 and 42. She wants to describe the distribution. Which description is most complete?

A complete description includes shape (left-skewed due to low outliers), center (around 85), spread, and unusual features (two outliers near 40).

Example: A sports analyst compared the points scored per game for two basketball teams over a season. Team A: mean 102, SD 15, symmetric. Team B: mean 98, SD 4, symmetric. Which team is more predictable in scoring?

Team B's standard deviation of 4 is much smaller than Team A's 15, meaning Team B's scores stay much closer to their mean of 98, making them more predictable.

summary_sections.title: Special Topics
summary_sections.content_html:
Important Concepts

For a right-skewed distribution, which measure of center is most appropriate to report

For skewed distributions, the median is preferred because it is not pulled by the extreme values in the tail, unlike the mean.

Which measure of spread is best paired with the median for a skewed distribution

The IQR is best paired with the median because both are resistant to outliers and extreme values, making them ideal for skewed distributions.

A distribution is described as 'left-skewed with a center of 82 and IQR of 15.' Which value is the center most likely

Since the distribution is skewed and the IQR is reported for spread, the center of 82 is most likely the median, as median and IQR are paired for skewed distributions.

A fitness tracker company collected step counts for 100 users on a typical weekday. The histogram shows a right-skewed distribution with a median of 6,500 steps and a mean of 7,800 steps. The company wants to advertise the 'average' user's step count. Which value should they use and why

In a right-skewed distribution, a few very active users inflate the mean. The median of 6,500 better represents the typical user since it is resistant to those extreme values.

A bakery tracked daily donut sales over a month. The data is left-skewed with a median of 120 and an IQR of 25. What is the best description of a typical day's sales

The median of 120 gives the center and the IQR of 25 means Q1 is about 120 - 12.5 = 107.5 and Q3 is about 120 + 12.5 = 132.5. This describes a typical day using context.

A hospital compared wait times for two emergency departments. ER-A: mean 45 min, SD 8 min, symmetric. ER-B: median 30 min, IQR 40 min, skewed right. Which ER has shorter typical wait times

ER-A's mean (45 min) represents its symmetric data well, and ER-B's median (30 min) represents its skewed data well. ER-B's typical wait time of 30 min is shorter than ER-A's 45 min.

An online retailer tracks daily revenue. The distribution is right-skewed with a median of $12,000 and IQR of $4,000. One day, revenue was $45,000 due to a flash sale. How should this day be described

$45,000 is far above the median of $12,000 and well beyond Q3 + 1.5 x IQR, making it an outlier. This unusual day should be noted when describing the distribution.

A nutritionist compared calorie counts of meals at two restaurants. Restaurant A: approximately normal, mean 650 cal, SD 80. Restaurant B: skewed right, median 500 cal, IQR 200. A customer wants a meal around 600 calories. Which restaurant is more likely to offer meals in that range

For Restaurant A, 600 is within one SD of the mean (650 - 80 = 570 to 650 + 80 = 730), where about 68% of meals fall. Restaurant B's median of 500 puts 600 further from the typical range.
